skill.name: godot-gdscript-patterns
skill.description: Use Godot 4 GDScript patterns for signals, scenes, state machines, resources, object pooling, save systems, and performance work.
skill.summary: Apply practical Godot 4 GDScript architecture patterns for production game systems.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution findings are false positives caused by Markdown code fences, inline resource references, and GDScript node path syntax, not executable Ruby or shell code. The weak-crypto, reconnaissance, and runtime-network detections are also false positives from ordinary Godot documentation text, public reference links, or local resource loading. The confirmed concern is a save-system example with local file operations and a hardcoded encryption passphrase that users could copy into production; no prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: Hardcoded Save Encryption Passphrase in Example
medium_findings.description: The save-system example defines ENCRYPTION_KEY as a fixed placeholder string and uses it for encrypted save reads and writes. This is not malicious, but users who copy the sample unchanged could ship predictable save-file encryption.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The fixed passphrase is directly shown and then passed into Godot encrypted file APIs. The context is educational documentation, so the risk is copy-paste misuse rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Local Save File Operations Require Review Before Reuse
low_findings.description: The examples read, write, and delete files under Godot user paths for high scores and save data. These are normal game-development patterns, but users should validate save paths and avoid adapting them to unrestricted paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file operations are clear and project-relevant, using Godot save locations. The concern is limited because the skill is documentation and the examples do not access arbitrary system paths.

low_findings.title: Static Command, Network, Crypto, and Recon Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The command-execution matches occur on Markdown fences, inline resource references, and GDScript node path syntax such as $Sprite2D. The network, weak-crypto, and reconnaissance matches are public documentation links, local ResourceLoader usage, or ordinary Godot text rather than suspicious runtime behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected locations are documentation syntax, GDScript examples, or public reference links. No shell invocation, credential transmission, or remote execution path was found in those contexts.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hardcoded Secret-Like Constant
dangerous_patterns.description: A save encryption key is represented as a constant placeholder in a reusable save-manager example. The sample should be treated as instructional and replaced with project-specific key handling.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The constant name and encrypted file API usage confirm secret-like behavior. The placeholder wording lowers malicious confidence but not the copy-paste risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Save File Deletion Example
dangerous_patterns.description: The save manager deletes a save path when a save exists. This is expected for a game save system, but should remain limited to Godot user storage.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The deletion call is explicit and scoped to the documented save path. There is no evidence of destructive behavior outside the game save example.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dynamic Scene Path Loading Example
dangerous_patterns.description: The scene manager loads a scene from a path argument. Projects should restrict callers to known project resources when adapting this pattern.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The path-based load is visible, but the example is for Godot PackedScene resources. Legitimate use is likely, with risk depending on how a project supplies scene paths.

content.value_statement: Godot projects can become hard to maintain when scenes, signals, and state are designed inconsistently. This skill provides practical GDScript patterns for reusable game systems and performance-aware architecture.
actual_capabilities: Explains Godot 4 architecture concepts for nodes, scenes, resources, signals, and groups.
Provides patterns for state machines, autoload singletons, event buses, and component systems.
Guides resource-based data models for weapons, character stats, and runtime copies.
Covers object pooling, scene transitions, save systems, and reusable game managers.
Highlights GDScript performance practices such as cached nodes, static typing, and reduced allocations.
limitations: It is a documentation skill and does not run or test Godot projects by itself.
Examples target Godot 4.x and may need changes for older engine versions.
It does not create art assets, animations, audio, or complete game design documents.
The save encryption example includes a placeholder key that must be replaced before production use.

use_cases.title: Plan a Maintainable Player Controller
use_cases.description: Break movement, combat, health, and animation behavior into patterns that fit a Godot 4 scene tree.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team GDScript Patterns
use_cases.description: Create shared conventions for signals, resources, state machines, and global managers across a project.

use_cases.title: Improve Performance-Sensitive Systems
use_cases.description: Review spawning, node lookup, scene loading, and allocation patterns before expanding a prototype.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Pattern
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning a new Godot feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: I am building a Godot 4 feature for [feature]. Recommend the best GDScript pattern from this skill and explain why.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Scene System
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate developer structuring scenes and scripts.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Godot 4 scene and script structure for [system]. Include signals, node ownership, resources, and validation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Existing Gameplay Code
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Godot GDScript design for [system]. Suggest refactors using state machines, components, resources, or autoloads.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Architecture and Performance
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Godot 4 architecture for maintainability, save safety, scene loading, signal coupling, and GDScript performance risks.

output_examples.input: Plan an enemy behavior system for a Godot action game.
output: Recommended pattern: state machine with separate idle, chase, attack, and stunned states.
Use signals for health and death events so combat code stays decoupled from animation and scoring.
Store enemy stats in resources and duplicate runtime values to avoid editing shared data.

output_examples.input: Improve frequent projectile spawning in a Godot 4 prototype.
output: Use an object pool for projectiles that are spawned often and returned after impact or timeout.
Cache node references and disable processing when pooled objects are inactive.
Measure results with the Godot profiler before increasing pool size.

output_examples.input: Review a save system pattern before production use.
output: Keep save paths under Godot user storage and avoid unrestricted absolute paths.
Replace placeholder encryption keys with project-specific handling.
Validate loaded data before applying it to scene nodes or player state.

best_practices: Start with the smallest pattern that solves the system, then add managers only when scope requires them.
Use signals and resources to reduce direct scene coupling and improve testability.
Review file handling, save data, and dynamic scene paths before copying examples into production.
anti_patterns: Do not turn every script into an autoload singleton.
Do not copy placeholder save encryption keys into a shipped game.
Do not perform repeated node lookups or allocations in hot frame loops.

faq.question: Which Godot version does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets Godot 4.x and GDScript patterns shown in the included playbook.

faq.question: Can it write complete Godot scripts?
faq.answer: It can guide script structure and patterns, but generated code should be tested inside Godot.

faq.question: Does it cover state machines?
faq.answer: Yes. The playbook includes a reusable state machine pattern and player state examples.

faq.question: Does it include save system guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes save and load examples, but the placeholder encryption key needs replacement.

faq.question: Is this useful for non-game Godot apps?
faq.answer: Some architecture patterns apply, but the examples focus on game systems and gameplay workflows.

faq.question: Does the skill require network access?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed files contain public reference links, but no runtime network client behavior was found.
